channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
item.description: AI Revolution: Microsoft is making Copilot Cowork generally available with usage-based billing and considering DeepSeek as a lower-cost model option on Azure. The shift represents a strategic move from single-assistant to multi-model AI agent platform capable of routing enterprise work across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork, and DeepSeek models.

item.title: Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
item.description: Google for Developers: Gemma 4 is a new version of Google's open-source language models optimized for on-device deployment. The 2B model now matches the performance of the previous year's 27B dense model. Gemma 4 is available through the Google AI Edge Gallery app and supports offline inference, agent skills framework, and upcoming MCP integration.

item.title: Stop Claude from forgetting anything
item.description: Jack Roberts: Hermes is a desktop tool that solves Claude's session memory loss by maintaining a persistent memory store across multiple projects and building a reusable skill library. It functions as a stateful companion layer that preserves context and details between sessions without requiring users to re-explain context for each new project.

item.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
item.description: AI Master: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI agent framework with nearly 200K GitHub stars that runs locally on Mac or Windows without paid subscriptions. It supports persistent memory, self-improvement through skill generation, Telegram integration, and cron job scheduling. The tutorial demonstrates end-to-end setup, model selection via OpenRouter, and practical use cases.

item.title: Matt Pocock’s Agentic Engineering Workflow (just copy him)
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses Matt Pocock's agentic engineering workflow and recommends adopting it as a best practice. The video features practical guidance on coding with AI and includes tooling recommendations such as SerpApi for web search integration.

item.title: Gemma Playground: AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google for Developers: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma running on Pixel phones via the Google AI Edge Gallery app. The app enables on-device multimodal tasks including image understanding, audio transcription, agent routing, and object recognition without internet connectivity.

item.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Addy Osmani outlines three core architectural patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy for efficient idle management, durable checkpointing for state persistence, and separated evaluation for robust execution. These patterns enable multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.

item.title: Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
item.description: Claude: Artifacts in Claude Code converts raw output such as data, mockups, and JSON into shareable visual pages. The feature is available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans and allows users to generate interactive artifacts in any session.

item.title: Antigravity SDK: Building a digital simulated world
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity SDK enables building multi-agent social simulations with virtual avatars interacting autonomously in simulated environments. A demonstration at Google I/O created a simulation inside a virtual space station with attendee avatars.

item.title: Enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors
item.description: Claude: Anthropic has launched enterprise-managed authentication for MCP connectors, allowing admins to authorize and authenticate connectors organization-wide through identity providers. This eliminates the need for individual employee setup and streamlines deployment at scale.

item.title: 3 takeaways on Software Ecology™️
item.description: Google for Developers: Google for Developers discusses three takeaways on Software Ecology and explores the implications of easy AI code generation for the future of software systems. The video features speaker Adam Bender and mentions Google AI products.

item.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: Video explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model as an introduction to how language models process tokens. Covers tokenization, embedding, vocabulary, cross entropy loss, softmax, optimization with AdamW, and backpropagation as prerequisites for understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

item.title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates practical workflows for deploying Antigravity 2.0, an AI coding agent, on enterprise codebases. The video covers seven tactics including encoding team practices into agent skills, automated test-driven self-correction, UI validation via headless browsers, and asynchronous background task offloading.

item.title: Your AI Agent Is Missing Half the Internet… Until Now (Agent-Reach)
item.description: Better Stack: Agent-Reach is an open source tool that enables AI agents to access real-time internet context across Western and Chinese platforms including Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu. It allows coding agents to retrieve discussions, social feedback, tutorials, GitHub issues, video comments, and non-English research from the internet in a single command.

item.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
item.description: AI Jason: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer and provides setup guidance. The tutorial includes a GitHub template repository and workshop for building AI agents using the Loop Engineer framework.

item.title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Model Context Protocol is an open standard that connects AI agents to external tools and live data sources. Google demonstrates MCP usage through its Agent Development Kit (ADK), showing how to integrate external data sources like Google Trends with AI agents using MCP servers.

item.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
item.description: AI Engineer: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks outlines five pillars for deploying AI agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The framework emerged from a retail bank engagement where six weeks of evaluation and tracing infrastructure were needed before model selection, and post-launch monitoring caught stale data issues when policy changes occurred.

item.title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
item.description: Code With Aarohi: A tutorial on building a multi-agent travel planning system that integrates LangGraph agents with MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. The system uses PostgreSQL for memory, Tavily Search, AviationStack and OpenWeatherMap APIs, and provides both terminal and Streamlit interfaces.

item.title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology outlines how AI agents operate in production systems with workflows, policies, and orchestration. The focus is on agent task coordination, rule enforcement, and alignment with human decision-making rather than introducing a specific new technique or tool.

item.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
item.description: AICodeKing: Z Code is a new coding agent from Z AI designed for GLM models, offering a free tier with 5 million tokens daily. It provides Codex-like functionality including code generation, project creation, and element inspection, with strong performance from the underlying GLM-5.2 model on coding and reasoning benchmarks.

item.title: 6 INSANE Claude Cowork Use Cases to Save Hours (AI Workflow Automation)
item.description: AI Master: A video tutorial demonstrating six workflow automation use cases built with Claude Cowork, including morning briefing automation, content generation, inbox triage, research report generation, dashboard creation, and mobile agent control.

item.title: When you realize that you’re the senior dev now.
item.description: Google for Developers: A YouTube video from Google for Developers featuring Pete King discussing the realization and experience of becoming a senior developer. The content uses humor to reflect on career advancement in software development.

item.title: How to build a custom vision agent
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Developer Expert John Capobianco demonstrates building a custom vision agent that captures live photos from a webcam and animates them. The system uses Gemini, Model Context Protocol, and processes natural language prompts and real-time American Sign Language conversations.

item.title: Meta head of product for 'AI for work' transformation is leaving company
item.description: donohoe: The head of product leading Meta's 'AI for work' transformation initiative is departing the company. This represents a notable shift in Meta's organizational structure around workplace AI efforts.

item.title: [x86] AI Compute Extensions (ACE) Specification
item.description: matt_d: x86 AI Compute Extensions (ACE) is a specification for CPU instruction set extensions designed to accelerate AI computations on x86 processors. The specification defines new instructions and capabilities for improving AI inference and training performance on standard CPUs.

item.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch provides guidance on disabling AI features in Google Docs, specifically the "write with Gemini" prompts that appear to users. The article offers instructions for turning off these AI-assisted writing suggestions.

item.title: Sovereign AI: Why Owning the Full Stack Is the New Strategic Imperative
item.description: HardAnchor: The author argues that vertical integration and full-stack ownership of AI infrastructure is becoming a strategic necessity. Control over chips, models, and deployment enables independence, security, and competitive advantage in the AI era.

item.title: Noam Shazeer is joining OpenAI
item.description: devanshp: Noam Shazeer, a prominent AI researcher previously at Google, is joining OpenAI. This move reflects competitive talent acquisition in the large language model space.

item.title: Noam Shazeer Joins OpenAI
item.description: lukasgross: Noam Shazeer has joined OpenAI. Shazeer previously founded Together AI and worked at Google Brain on large language models.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Anthropic curbs ignite AI debate in India — efforts 'too slow, way too small' - CNBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has implemented operational curbs in India, sparking debate about the adequacy of India's AI governance response. Critics argue the nation's efforts to address these restrictions are insufficiently fast and too limited in scope.

item.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
item.description: Cole Medin: AI engineers use loop-based orchestration to automate agent prompting without manual intervention. The approach chains agents together with an orchestrator that spawns parallel workers for tasks, storing observations in a database for monitoring and reliability. This technique reduces manual prompting but increases token costs and can compound hallucinations without proper observability harnesses.

item.title: GLM-5.2 is probably the most powerful text-only open weights LLM
item.description: Simon Willison: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, a 753B parameter text-only open weights LLM with 40 active parameters via Mixture of Experts and a 1 million token context window. It ranks first on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and second on the Code Arena WebDev leaderboard, though it generates significantly more output tokens than comparable models.

item.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
item.description: AI Revolution: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight coding model designed for coding agents working through real codebases. The model is positioned as efficient and competitive with closed AI coding systems.

item.title: Towards autonomous medical artificial intelligence agents - Nature
item.description: Google News AI: Nature publishes an article discussing the trajectory toward autonomous AI agents in medical applications. The piece appears to explore current progress and future directions for AI systems capable of autonomous decision-making and action in healthcare contexts.

item.title: French president urges US to share cutting-edge AI and democracies to cooperate on regulation - AP News
item.description: Google News AI: The French president called on the US to share advanced AI technology and advocated for democratic nations to collaborate on AI regulation. The statement reflects concerns about equitable access to AI capabilities and coordinated governance approaches among democracies.

item.title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models – Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI [video]
item.description: Topfi: Maxime Labonne from Liquid AI presents lessons learned from training frontier small models. The talk covers insights into the training methodology and best practices for developing efficient small language models.

item.title: Toward an O*NET for AI R&D
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI proposes adapting the O*NET occupational classification system to create a systematic framework for tracking and categorizing AI research automation. This would enable structured measurement of which research tasks are being automated and at what scale.

item.title: A robot is sprinting towards you. Do you want it running on Claude or Grok?
item.description: Usu: A provocative question comparing Claude and Grok as foundation models for robot control. The framing suggests implicit debate about safety, reliability, or performance differences between the two systems in a high-stakes scenario.

item.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Roelof Botha, former Sequoia Capital leader, joined SpaceX's board of directors, filling an existing vacancy days after SpaceX's IPO, which was noted as the largest IPO ever.

item.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Snap unveiled new AR glasses at a high price point, which was followed by a decline in the company's stock price. TechCrunch characterizes the glasses as "ridiculously expensive" and notes the negative market reaction to the product debut.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.description: TechCrunch AI: NEA investor Tiffany Luck discusses how enterprises are struggling to measure AI return on investment. After aggressive "tokenmaxxing" spending earlier in the year, companies like Uber exhausted budgets quickly, leading to cost constraints and pullbacks on AI tool licenses.

item.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
item.description: droidjj: A discussion on Hacker News by droidjj examining how agentic coding systems interact with expert specialization. The piece explores the thesis that even as autonomous coding agents advance, deep expertise in specific domains continues to provide durable competitive advantages.

item.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts presents a seven-level framework for Hermes agent development, progressing from basic prompting to fully agentic operating systems. The framework emphasizes orchestrating multiple LLMs (Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, Grok) with skills, sub-agents, and scheduled tasks to automate administrative and software development work.

item.title: The hacker sent by Anthropic to calm the government's nerves about AI safety
item.description: Brajeshwar: A commentary on Anthropic's effort to engage with government regarding AI safety concerns. The title's framing with "hacker sent by Anthropic" suggests skepticism about the motivations and effectiveness of the company's government relations strategy on safety issues.

item.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
item.description: TechCrunch AI: World leaders including French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised concerns at the G7 summit that the U.S. could unilaterally cut off access to American AI systems. The Anthropic service outage made this vulnerability concrete for international stakeholders worried about dependence on U.S. AI infrastructure.

item.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL. The tool helps developers migrate database schemas and explains conversion processes like converting user-defined types to PostgreSQL DOMAINs.

item.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
item.description: Jack Roberts: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into the Microsoft ecosystem that proactively reads Teams, email, and calendar data to prepare meeting materials and handle tasks before users initiate requests. It operates as an autonomous assistant requiring minimal user direction.

item.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
item.description: Zhenghao Xing, Ruiyang Xu, Yuxuan Wang: OmniAgent is an omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle to reduce computational cost from video duration. It introduces Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning via best-of-N trajectory synthesis and Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA for turn-level credit assignment. The approach exhibits positive test-time scaling and achieves state-of-the-art results on video understanding benchmarks.

item.title: Beyond the Current Observation: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Controllable Non-Markov Games
item.description: Shengyuan Ding, Xilin Wei, Xinyu Fang: RNG-Bench is a benchmark suite that isolates multimodal language models' ability to reconstruct past observations and act on them in non-Markovian environments. It includes Matching Pairs and 3D Maze games with controlled difficulty axes, a head-to-head duel protocol, and a Memory Gap metric that separates forgetting from poor action selection.

item.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
item.description: Yingshan Susan Wang, Cedegao E. Zhang, Linlu Qiu: Turing-RL is a reinforcement learning approach that trains user simulator LLMs to produce responses indistinguishable from real users by optimizing a discriminative Turing reward, rather than matching ground truth responses. The method is evaluated on conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion tasks, outperforming baselines on both LLM and human evaluation metrics.
